text: we should update planning guidance on folder/file taxonomy and make seams based on likely maintenance boundaries, not nouns or some other garbage for micro splitting
create a local tui-playground extension that essentially works as a way for me to browse custom TUI components and validate what I like.

I think we need to supersede greps if any file from inside of it is red and the way we get around informing the agent that that's going to happen is that after a grep if it reads any file from within that grep it needs to make sure to batch read all files because that grep is going to disappear and it won't have the information from it anymore.
tau edit should use force reads via an event that forces reads into context window instead of doing something hidden. this is so that they can se superceeded with working-memeory system

Need a system to know like probably like a read digest or something like that to know if a file that is in the context window has you know that has been read has had its digest changed after the user hit submit so it's not the same as what it previously was because maybe the user manually edited it outside of the window the agent should know it needs to reread that file with a slight nudge when a user submits their prompt.

Should we do prompt caching pings like a and also maybe show the user a warning and maybe just a permanent countdown timer since their last message before the cache will bust? And is any of this taken into account with cache hits that are being rendered by Pi or my custom footer?

Is that is cache hit already kind of pulling from the provider to know whether or not cash was actually hit and also we need to figure out what the cache semantics are for the various models that I use. ChatGPT 5.5, Opus 4.8, GLM5.2

What about something like a smart compaction? If we keep with something like postures, what about after planning, if the agent wants to switch over to acting, it needs to have a plan file and it should essentially mark files to be re-read and it should completely purge context history and start with in the way compaction does but like it's automatic it doesn't send to an LM just basically does the same thing compaction does but without putting in the summary basically and well it auto-generates a summary based on like this whole concept of the forget mechanism but it's like essentially it needs to mark things as keep, whatever would be better, forget or keep.

It could do both, either or whichever it thinks is more efficient in the particular use case and based on that it keeps those exact context items like reads that it should keep so it doesn't have to read them again through another tool call, but it gets rid of everything that it shouldn't have anymore.

I want to update the Lyle identity to be more ponytail-like and and more kind of role-play where he kind of announces as if he's like talking out loud, like, I should probably go read this file. Interesting. Oh no, this one shit. Didn't have anything in it.

Stuff like that. Acknowledge it. I think those kind of acknowledgements will help it use the forget tool better.
figure out how pi /plan example works and switch posture to something like that plus ponytail
silent running should run before the agent ends the turn so I dont here the turn end sound before the ailent runner finishes

build a session archive feature that allows you to keep and select the number of or the individual sessions you want to keep and it moves the rest into an archive folder, but then it's a tab view as well, so you can go into the archive folder and unarchive anything, but the archive doesn't load until you go into it, so because we're trying to make things more more performant and and probably also want like auto stashing as well so that it only ever keeps like the last 25 sessions or something like that they automatically move to archive and again because it's you know archive it's recoverable but then we need to be able to easily delete things from the archive.
need to update silent runner because agent is still manually running checks
update tau-new to allow you to support tagging reference examples and inform the agent to make decisions based on its context. Should also auto inject docs based on what you are creating.
subagents scout mode needs to provide files and ranges for targeted lookup to save context.
we need whole file structural maps when agent tries to read whole file
session cost should include subagent executions
